regimeSub: Warsh-led Fed scraps cutting bias and threatens hikes as CPI hits 4.2% 3-yr high; Iran deal kills the oil tail.
themes: Iran-US deal slashes Brent to 3-month lows -> energy disinflation impulse, but headline CPI already at 4.2%
Warsh's first FOMC removes easing bias -> bear-steepening, Dow -500 even as oil relief lifts risk
Negative-to-thin real policy rate (3.63% vs 4.2% CPI) -> Fed boxed into hike risk into September
Yen at 160 and KRW at 1522 -> Asia FX stress masked by record Nikkei/KOSPI melt-up on AI

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Policy rate 3.63% trails 4.2% CPI; real policy rate still negative, fueling Fed's hawkish tilt.

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: +38bps and bear-steepening post-Warsh; market pricing hikes, not cuts, into year-end.

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: Still tight; Iran de-escalation and SPX 7420 keep credit complacent despite yield surge.

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: 18.4, calm; oil-shock premium drained by Iran deal, but Fed hawkishness an unpriced risk.

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: $78 at 3-month lows on US-Iran agreement; geopolitical tail cut, disinflationary going forward.

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: $4,340, off $4,475 highs as war premium fades; still bid on negative real rates and Fed credibility doubts.

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: 1,522 weak won despite KOSPI 8,955 record; BOK-Fed gap and yen at 160 pressure Korean FX.

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: Nvidia/Broadcom/Micron led Nasdaq drop on yield spike; AI leadership wobbling as discount rate rises.

headline: Oil's geopolitical tail is gone after the US-Iran deal, but a 4.2% inflation print and a newly hawkish Warsh Fed have flipped the rate path from cuts to hikes—bonds sell off, equities split, and stagflation risk is back on the table.
